methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $700M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: KoBold Metals
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $2B+

private.name: Electric Hydrogen
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Amogy is a Series C climate and energy company currently valued at $700M post-money, having raised $300M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of nine same-sector, same-to-adjacent-stage private and public companies, including KoBold Metals ($2.0B Series C), Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B Series C), Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B Series B), and Redwood Materials ($6.0B Series D). One public comparable exists in the set.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a typical 1.7x multiplier for Series C-to-next-stage advancement, implies $1.2B. Peer-median analysis across the nine-company Climate & Energy cohort yields $500M, with the peer set ranging from $120M to $6.0B. The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in technology maturity and deployment timeline within the frontier-energy sector.

Triangulation of both methods produces a low of $425M, median of $845M, and high of $1.4B. The current $700M valuation sits modestly below the median implied value, suggesting modest upside if execution de-risks technology and market adoption. Key uncertainties include the relative stage positioning of Amogy within the peer set, timeline to next financing event, and technology-specific commercialization risk endemic to pre-revenue frontier-energy companies.
